articles.headline: Antyodaya Diwas: Centre Launches 'Sarvodaya Kalyan Abhiyan' for Integrated Social Welfare
articles.explainer:
• Coinciding with the birth anniversary of Pandit Deendayal Upadhyaya, the government launched a new integrated welfare program.
• The 'Sarvodaya Kalyan Abhiyan' aims to converge existing flagship schemes like PM-KISAN, Ayushman Bharat, and PM-AWAS.
• Its core focus is on achieving last-mile delivery and ensuring benefits reach the 'last person' (Antyodaya principle).
• The initiative emphasizes technology-driven monitoring, Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT), and community participation for efficient implementation.
• Special attention will be given to identified aspirational districts and vulnerable sections of society to achieve saturation coverage.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
Philosophy of Deendayal Upadhyaya
prelimsPoints: Antyodaya Diwas is celebrated on September 25th.
It marks the birth anniversary of Pandit Deendayal Upadhyaya.
'Sarvodaya Kalyan Abhiyan' aims for convergence of welfare schemes.
Key principles: Antyodaya, last-mile delivery, technology integration.
mainsPoints: Discuss the relevance of Antyodaya principle in contemporary welfare policies.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in converging multiple social welfare schemes.
Evaluate the role of technology and DBT in enhancing the efficiency and transparency of welfare delivery.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of people from diverse backgrounds receiving benefits from integrated government schemes, symbolizing 'Antyodaya'.
articles.trivia: Pandit Deendayal Upadhyaya was one of the most important ideologues of the Bharatiya Jana Sangh, the precursor to the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP).

articles.headline: India Advocates for Climate Justice and Reformed Multilateralism at UN General Assembly
articles.explainer:
• India's External Affairs Minister addressed the 80th session of the UN General Assembly (UNGA), emphasizing the need for climate justice.
• The address highlighted India's commitment to sustainable development goals while urging developed nations to fulfill their financial and technological commitments.
• India reiterated its call for a reformed multilateralism, advocating for a more representative and equitable United Nations Security Council (UNSC).
• Concerns were raised about rising geopolitical tensions, cross-border terrorism, and the need for collective action to maintain global peace and stability.
• The Minister underscored India's role as a voice for the Global South and its contributions to addressing global challenges.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: UNGA is the main deliberative organ of the UN.
India consistently advocates for UNSC reforms.
Climate justice emphasizes equitable burden-sharing for climate action.
India is a prominent voice for the Global South.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's persistent demand for reforms in multilateral institutions, particularly the UN, and its implications for global governance.
Discuss India's stance on climate change, focusing on the concept of climate justice and its challenges for developing nations.
Evaluate India's role as a leader of the Global South in shaping international discourse on critical issues.
articles.imageDescription: External Affairs Minister speaking at the podium of the United Nations General Assembly hall.
articles.trivia: The UN General Assembly convenes annually in September for its regular session, with general debate usually held in the first week.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Key Policy Rates Amidst Global Headwinds, Focus on Inflation Management
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged.
• The decision was influenced by persistent global economic uncertainties, geopolitical tensions, and domestic inflationary pressures.
• RBI signaled a cautious approach, emphasizing a data-driven strategy to balance growth objectives with inflation control.
• The central bank highlighted the need to monitor food inflation and crude oil prices, which pose significant risks to the economic outlook.
• Liquidity measures were also discussed, with the RBI reaffirming its commitment to maintaining adequate liquidity in the financial system.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC determines key policy rates.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends to commercial banks.
Inflation targeting is a primary objective of MPC.
Global factors like crude oil prices impact domestic inflation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the current challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in managing supply-side inflation and external shocks.
Evaluate the role of the MPC in maintaining financial stability in the Indian economy.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai with a graph showing stable interest rates.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in India in 2016 and consists of six members, with the RBI Governor as its ex-officio chairperson.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Lays Down Comprehensive Guidelines for Admissibility of Digital Evidence
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India issued a landmark judgment, establishing detailed guidelines for the admissibility and evidentiary value of digital records in criminal and civil cases.
• The ruling aims to address the complexities arising from the increasing use of electronic evidence and ensure fair trial principles.
• Guidelines cover aspects such as the collection, preservation, authentication, and presentation of digital evidence, including mobile phone data, emails, and CCTV footage.
• The court emphasized the need for specialized training for law enforcement agencies and judicial officers in handling digital forensics.
• This decision is expected to streamline legal proceedings and enhance the reliability of digital evidence in courts across the country.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court ruling on digital evidence admissibility.
Key aspects: collection, preservation, authentication.
Relevant laws: Indian Evidence Act, IT Act.
Impacts criminal and civil cases.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by digital evidence in the Indian legal system and how the Supreme Court's guidelines address them.
Analyze the implications of this judgment for law enforcement agencies and the need for capacity building in digital forensics.
Evaluate the balance struck by the court between technological advancements and ensuring fair trial principles.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal books with a blurred background of a digital screen displaying code.
articles.trivia: Section 65B of the Indian Evidence Act, 1872, specifically deals with the admissibility of electronic records as evidence.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Deploys Next-Generation Weather Satellite, Enhancing Climate Monitoring
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched and deployed a state-of-the-art geostationary weather satellite.
• The satellite, designated 'Kalpana-3', is equipped with advanced imaging and atmospheric sounding instruments for improved weather forecasting and climate studies.
• It will provide high-resolution data for monitoring cyclones, monsoons, cloud patterns, and sea surface temperatures.
• This mission significantly boosts India's capability in disaster management, agriculture planning, and understanding regional climate change impacts.
• The launch was carried out using ISRO's Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle (GSLV) from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Space technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO launched 'Kalpana-3' weather satellite.
Uses GSLV launcher.
Provides data for weather, climate, disaster management.
Geostationary orbit for continuous coverage.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of advanced weather satellites for India's agricultural sector and disaster preparedness.
Analyze how ISRO's capabilities in space technology contribute to national development and international cooperation.
Evaluate the role of space-based observations in understanding and mitigating the impacts of climate change in the Indian subcontinent.
articles.imageDescription: A GSLV rocket launching into space, with a satellite orbiting Earth in the background.
articles.trivia: Kalpana-1 was India's first dedicated meteorological satellite, launched in 2002 and named after Indian-American astronaut Kalpana Chawla.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in Tokyo, with discussions centered on enhancing cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Both nations reaffirmed their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, emphasizing adherence to international law and peaceful resolution of disputes.
• Key areas of cooperation included defense and security, infrastructure development, clean energy, and resilient supply chains.
• Discussions also covered regional security challenges, including maritime security and counter-terrorism efforts.
• The dialogue aimed to strengthen the 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership' between India and Japan, fostering stability and prosperity in the region.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India and Japan hold annual Strategic Dialogue.
Focus on Indo-Pacific region.
Areas of cooperation: defense, infrastructure, clean energy.
Both are members of Quad grouping.
mainsPoints: Examine the significance of the India-Japan Special Strategic and Global Partnership in promoting stability and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss how India's Act East Policy aligns with Japan's vision for a free and open Indo-Pacific.
Analyze the potential for enhanced economic and security cooperation between India and Japan to counter emerging geopolitical challenges.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and Japanese flags displayed side-by-side during a diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: Japan is a significant investor in India's infrastructure, notably involved in projects like the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.

articles.headline: National Mission on Natural Farming to Expand Coverage to New States
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare announced the expansion of the National Mission on Natural Farming (NMNF) to several new states.
• The mission aims to promote chemical-free and sustainable agricultural practices, reducing input costs for farmers and improving soil health.
• Key components include farmer training, establishment of model natural farming villages, and certification of natural farm produce.
• The expansion is expected to boost organic and natural farming, contributing to food security and environmental sustainability.
• Emphasis will be placed on integrating traditional Indian farming practices with modern scientific validation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Agriculture
Government policies and interventions
prelimsPoints: National Mission on Natural Farming (NMNF) promotes chemical-free farming.
Aims to reduce input costs and improve soil health.
Includes farmer training and certification.
Contributes to environmental sustainability.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential benefits and challenges of scaling up natural farming practices across diverse agro-climatic zones in India.
Analyze how natural farming can contribute to India's climate change mitigation and adaptation goals.
Evaluate the role of government policies and farmer awareness in transitioning towards sustainable agricultural systems.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer working in a lush green field, symbolizing natural and sustainable agriculture.
articles.trivia: Zero Budget Natural Farming (ZBNF) is a popular form of natural farming promoted by agricultural scientist Subhash Palekar.

articles.headline: Joint Parliamentary Committee Submits Report on Digital Personal Data Protection Bill
articles.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) submitted its report on the highly anticipated Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2025.
• The report includes recommendations for strengthening data privacy safeguards, defining consent mechanisms, and establishing a robust data protection authority.
• Key discussions revolved around the balance between individual privacy rights, government access to data, and the needs of businesses.
• The proposed legislation aims to replace the existing IT Act provisions related to data protection and establish a comprehensive legal framework.
• Experts anticipate the bill to undergo further parliamentary debate before its eventual enactment, marking a significant step for India's digital governance.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Role of Parliament and State Legislatures
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: Digital Personal Data Protection Bill aims to protect privacy.
JPC submitted its report on the bill.
Will replace parts of the IT Act.
Proposes a Data Protection Authority.
mainsPoints: Analyze the importance of a robust data protection framework in the context of India's growing digital economy and citizen rights.
Discuss the challenges in balancing individual privacy, national security, and economic interests in data governance.
Evaluate the recommendations of the JPC and their potential impact on India's digital landscape and fundamental rights.
articles.imageDescription: A blurred image of parliamentary proceedings with a focus on a document titled 'Data Protection Bill'.
articles.trivia: The Supreme Court's Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: India on Track to Exceed Renewable Energy Targets by 2030, Says Ministry Report
articles.explainer:
• A new report by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy indicates that India is well-positioned to exceed its ambitious renewable energy targets for 2030.
• Significant progress has been made in solar and wind energy capacity additions, driven by policy support and private sector investments.
• The report highlights the role of schemes like the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar PV manufacturing in boosting domestic capabilities.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, energy storage, and land acquisition, but government initiatives are addressing these.
• Achieving these targets is crucial for India's commitment to climate change mitigation and reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Government policies and interventions
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India aims for significant renewable energy capacity by 2030.
Progress driven by solar and wind energy.
PLI scheme supports domestic manufacturing.
Challenges include grid integration and storage.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key policy initiatives and technological advancements that are driving India's renewable energy transition.
Discuss the challenges associated with large-scale renewable energy integration into the national grid and potential solutions.
Evaluate the economic and environmental benefits of India's aggressive renewable energy targets for its long-term sustainable development.
articles.imageDescription: A large solar power plant stretching to the horizon under a clear sky.
articles.trivia: India's target under the Paris Agreement is to achieve about 50% cumulative electric power installed capacity from non-fossil fuel-based energy resources by 2030.

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Garners International Interest at G20 Side Event
articles.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, including Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, was showcased at a G20 side event.
• Several developing nations expressed keen interest in adopting similar frameworks to accelerate their digital transformation journeys.
• The DPI framework has been lauded for its ability to foster financial inclusion, streamline public service delivery, and boost economic growth.
• Discussions focused on the open-source nature of India's DPI, its interoperability, and the potential for cross-border applications.
• India reaffirmed its commitment to sharing its DPI expertise and experience with other countries, promoting digital cooperation.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
E-governance
prelimsPoints: Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker are components of India's DPI.
DPI model is gaining international interest.
Promotes financial inclusion and public service delivery.
India committed to sharing DPI expertise globally.
mainsPoints: Discuss how India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) has revolutionized financial inclusion and public service delivery in the country.
Analyze the potential for India's DPI model to be adopted by other developing nations and its implications for global digital cooperation.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities associated with scaling up DPI solutions while ensuring data privacy and security.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract representation of interconnected digital networks with icons of Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
articles.trivia: Unified Payments Interface (UPI) processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month in India, highlighting its massive adoption.

articles.headline: New Education Policy: Focus on Foundational Literacy and Numeracy, Implementation Challenges Persist
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education reviewed the progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, with a particular focus on foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN).
• While significant strides have been made in curriculum development and teacher training, challenges in effective implementation at the grassroots level persist.
• Issues such as inadequate infrastructure, teacher vacancies, and digital divide in rural areas were highlighted as key hurdles.
• The government emphasized the need for greater state-level ownership and community participation to achieve NEP's transformative goals.
• Efforts are underway to strengthen early childhood care and education (ECCE) and integrate vocational education from an early stage.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 focuses on FLN and ECCE.
Implementation challenges include infrastructure and teacher vacancies.
Aims to integrate vocational education.
Ministry of Education reviews progress.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the progress and persistent challenges in implementing the National Education Policy 2020, particularly concerning foundational literacy and numeracy.
Discuss the role of state governments and local communities in ensuring the successful realization of NEP's objectives.
Analyze how the NEP addresses issues of equity and access in education, especially in the context of the digital divide.
articles.imageDescription: Children in a classroom learning, symbolizing foundational education initiatives.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India, replacing the thirty-four-year-old National Policy on Education (NPE), 1986.

articles.headline: India-Central Asia Summit Emphasizes Connectivity and Regional Security Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• Leaders from India and five Central Asian nations convened for a virtual summit, focusing on enhancing regional connectivity and security cooperation.
• Discussions centered on optimizing the International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC) and Chabahar Port for seamless trade.
• Both sides expressed concerns over the situation in Afghanistan and its implications for regional stability, pledging coordinated efforts against terrorism.
• Economic cooperation, including trade, investment, and energy partnerships, was identified as a key pillar of the 'extended neighborhood' policy.
• The summit concluded with a joint declaration reaffirming commitment to a strategic partnership based on mutual trust and shared interests.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-Central Asia Summit focuses on connectivity and security.
INSTC and Chabahar Port are key connectivity projects.
Concerns over Afghanistan's stability.
Part of India's 'extended neighborhood' policy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of Central Asia for India's economic and security interests.
Discuss the role of connectivity projects like INSTC and Chabahar Port in strengthening India's engagement with Central Asia.
Evaluate the challenges posed by the situation in Afghanistan to regional security and India's efforts to counter them in cooperation with Central Asian nations.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing the International North-South Transport Corridor connecting India with Central Asia.
articles.trivia: The INSTC is a 7,200-km-long multi-modal transport corridor for moving freight between India, Iran, Afghanistan, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Russia, Central Asia, and Europe.

articles.headline: National Cybersecurity Strategy: Government Prioritizes Critical Infrastructure Protection
articles.explainer:
• The National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025 is being actively implemented, with a strong focus on protecting critical information infrastructure (CII).
• Government agencies are collaborating with private sector entities to enhance resilience against sophisticated cyber threats.
• Key initiatives include capacity building for cybersecurity professionals, promoting indigenous cybersecurity products, and fostering international cooperation.
• A new framework for mandatory cyber audits of government and critical private sector entities has been introduced.
• The strategy aims to create a secure and resilient cyberspace for citizens and businesses, safeguarding national security and economic stability.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Internal Security
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
keyTerms: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsPoints: National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025 focuses on CII protection.
Aims for collaboration between public and private sectors.
Includes capacity building and indigenous product promotion.
Mandatory cyber audits framework introduced.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cyber threats and their implications for India's critical infrastructure and national security.
Analyze the key pillars of India's National Cybersecurity Strategy and the challenges in its effective implementation.
Evaluate the importance of public-private partnerships and international cooperation in building a robust cybersecurity ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract digital network lines protecting a silhouette of a city skyline, symbolizing cybersecurity.
articles.trivia: The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) is the nodal agency for responding to computer security incidents as and when they occur.

articles.headline: Ganges River Dolphin Conservation Efforts Intensify, Focus on Habitat Protection
articles.explainer:
• Conservation efforts for the endangered Ganges River Dolphin have been intensified, with a renewed focus on habitat protection and pollution control.
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) and other agencies are implementing measures to reduce industrial and municipal pollution in key dolphin habitats.
• Community engagement programs are being launched to raise awareness and involve local fishermen in conservation activities.
• Research initiatives are also underway to better understand the dolphin population dynamics, health, and migration patterns.
• The Ganges River Dolphin, India's national aquatic animal, faces threats from habitat degradation, poaching, and accidental entanglement in fishing nets.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Ganges River Dolphin is India's National Aquatic Animal.
Conservation efforts focus on habitat and pollution.
NMCG is involved in conservation.
Threats include pollution, poaching, entanglement.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Ganges River Dolphin and the various threats it faces in its natural habitat.
Analyze the effectiveness of current conservation strategies, including government initiatives and community participation, in protecting this endangered species.
Suggest further measures that can be adopted to ensure the long-term survival of the Ganges River Dolphin amidst increasing anthropogenic pressures.
articles.imageDescription: A Ganges River Dolphin swimming in clear river water, partially visible above the surface.
articles.trivia: The Ganges River Dolphin is one of the few freshwater dolphin species in the world and is functionally blind, relying on echolocation to navigate and hunt.

articles.headline: India's Tourism Sector Unveils New Strategy for Post-Pandemic Revival and Sustainable Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism launched a comprehensive strategy aimed at revitalizing India's tourism sector and promoting sustainable growth.
• The strategy focuses on developing niche tourism products, promoting responsible tourism practices, and enhancing digital marketing efforts.
• Special emphasis is placed on rural tourism, eco-tourism, and spiritual circuits to diversify offerings and create local employment.
• Initiatives include skill development programs for tourism professionals and improving last-mile connectivity to tourist destinations.
• The goal is to position India as a premier global tourist destination while ensuring environmental and cultural preservation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Government policies and interventions
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of India's tourism sector to drive economic growth and employment generation, especially in rural areas.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Indian tourism industry in the post-pandemic era and how the new strategy addresses them.
Evaluate the importance of sustainable and responsible tourism practices in preserving India's cultural and natural heritage.
articles.imageDescription: A collage of diverse Indian tourist attractions, including historical monuments, natural landscapes, and cultural festivals.
articles.trivia: The Incredible India campaign, launched by the Ministry of Tourism, has been highly successful in promoting India as a global tourist destination.

articles.headline: Indian Army Accelerates Modernization and Indigenization Efforts with New Procurement Deals
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Army announced several new procurement deals aimed at accelerating its modernization drive and boosting indigenous defense manufacturing.
• The acquisitions include advanced artillery systems, surveillance drones, and specialized equipment for mountain warfare, aligning with 'Make in India' initiatives.
• Emphasis is placed on acquiring cutting-edge technology from domestic manufacturers and reducing reliance on foreign imports.
• The move is part of a broader strategy to enhance the Army's operational capabilities, particularly along disputed borders.
• These efforts also aim to strengthen India's defense industrial base and create employment opportunities within the country.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Indian Army focuses on modernization and indigenization.
New deals for artillery, drones, mountain warfare equipment.
Aligned with 'Make in India' in defense.
Aims to reduce foreign import reliance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic imperative behind the Indian Army's accelerated modernization and indigenization efforts.
Analyze the impact of 'Make in India' initiative on India's defense industrial base and its implications for national security.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in achieving self-reliance in defense manufacturing, particularly for advanced military technologies.
articles.imageDescription: Modern Indian Army tanks and artillery positioned in a rugged mountainous terrain.
articles.trivia: The 'Make in India' initiative for defense aims to boost domestic manufacturing and reduce India's reliance on imported defense equipment, making it a net exporter.

articles.headline: Indus Water Treaty: Permanent Indus Commission Meeting Concludes with Technical Discussions
articles.explainer:
• The annual meeting of the Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) between India and Pakistan concluded with constructive technical discussions.
• Delegates reviewed hydrological data, flood forecasting mechanisms, and discussed various projects on the Indus basin rivers.
• While no major breakthroughs were announced, both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT).
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank, governs the sharing of waters of the Indus River System between the two countries.
• Such meetings are crucial for maintaining dialogue and resolving minor technical disputes related to water sharing, despite broader bilateral tensions.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Indus Water Treaty (IWT) between India and Pakistan.
Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) meets annually.
IWT allocates Eastern and Western Rivers.
World Bank is a signatory to the treaty.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and key provisions of the Indus Water Treaty and its significance in India-Pakistan relations.
Analyze the role of the Permanent Indus Commission in resolving water-related disputes and maintaining cooperation despite political tensions.
Evaluate the challenges to the IWT in the face of climate change, increasing water demand, and hydropower development.
articles.imageDescription: Map of the Indus River System showing the allocation of rivers between India and Pakistan.
articles.trivia: The Indus Water Treaty, signed in 1960, is considered one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, having survived multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

articles.headline: PM-Vishwakarma Scheme Empowers Artisans and Craftsmen, Boosts Traditional Skills
articles.explainer:
• The PM-Vishwakarma Kaushal Samman Yojana, launched to support traditional artisans and craftsmen, shows promising progress in its initial phase.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, skill training, modern tools, and market linkages to 'Vishwakarmas' across 18 traditional trades.
• It aims to preserve India's rich heritage of artisanal skills while integrating these communities into the formal economy.
• Beneficiaries are receiving easy access to credit, digital payment incentives, and branding support for their products.
• The initiative is expected to boost local economies, promote 'Vocal for Local', and empower marginalized sections of society.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
articles.source: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
prelimsPoints: PM-Vishwakarma scheme for traditional artisans/craftsmen.
Provides financial aid, training, tools, market linkages.
Covers 18 traditional trades.
Aims to preserve heritage and boost local economy.
mainsPoints: Discuss how the PM-Vishwakarma scheme contributes to preserving India's traditional crafts and empowering the artisan community.
Analyze the role of such schemes in promoting 'Vocal for Local' and fostering inclusive economic growth.
Evaluate the challenges faced by traditional artisans and how government interventions can effectively address issues of market access, credit, and skill upgradation.
articles.imageDescription: An Indian artisan meticulously crafting a traditional product, surrounded by tools and raw materials.
articles.trivia: The term 'Vishwakarma' refers to the divine architect and craftsman, symbolizing the reverence for traditional skills and craftsmanship in Indian culture.

articles.headline: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme Boosts Early-Stage Innovation Across Sectors
articles.explainer:
• The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) has significantly contributed to nurturing early-stage startups and fostering innovation across diverse sectors.
• The scheme provides financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, and market-entry.
• It has particularly benefited startups in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities, promoting a more equitable distribution of entrepreneurial activity.
• Focus areas include deep technology, clean energy, healthcare, and agritech, aligning with national development priorities.
• The scheme is crucial for bridging the funding gap for nascent ventures, enabling them to scale and contribute to job creation and economic growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Government policies and interventions
articles.source: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
prelimsPoints: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) supports early-stage startups.
Provides financial assistance for proof of concept, prototype.
Benefits startups in Tier 2/3 cities.
Focus on deep tech, clean energy, healthcare, agritech.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of government-backed seed funding schemes in fostering a vibrant startup ecosystem in India.
Analyze the impact of the Startup India Seed Fund Scheme on promoting innovation and entrepreneurship beyond metropolitan areas.
Evaluate the challenges faced by early-stage startups in India and how such schemes address the funding gap and market access issues.
articles.imageDescription: A group of young entrepreneurs collaborating in a modern office space, symbolizing startup culture.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top three startup ecosystems globally, with a rapidly growing number of unicorns (startups valued at over $1 billion).

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Initiates Major Conservation Project at Ancient Harappan Site
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) commenced a large-scale conservation and restoration project at a prominent ancient Harappan site in Gujarat.
• The project aims to preserve the structural integrity of the excavated remains, protect artifacts, and enhance visitor experience.
• Advanced archaeological techniques, including laser scanning and drone mapping, are being employed for detailed documentation and planning.
• The initiative is part of India's broader commitment to protecting its rich cultural heritage and promoting archaeological research.
• The site, known for its unique urban planning and water management systems, offers crucial insights into the Indus Valley Civilization.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: ASI initiated conservation at a Harappan site in Gujarat.
Uses advanced techniques like laser scanning.
Harappan sites known for urban planning, water systems.
Indus Valley Civilization is one of the world's oldest.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Harappan sites in understanding India's ancient history and cultural heritage.
Analyze the challenges involved in the conservation and preservation of ancient archaeological sites in India.
Evaluate the role of modern technology and community involvement in enhancing the protection and accessibility of such heritage sites.
articles.imageDescription: Aerial view of an ancient Harappan ruin site, showcasing its distinct urban layout.
articles.trivia: Dholavira, a Harappan city in Gujarat, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and is particularly known for its sophisticated ancient water management system.

highlights: Government launches 'Sarvodaya Kalyan Abhiyan' on Antyodaya Diwas, integrating social welfare schemes for last-mile delivery.
India advocates for climate justice and reformed multilateralism at the 80th session of the UN General Assembly.
RBI maintains key policy rates amidst global economic uncertainties, prioritizing inflation management.
Supreme Court issues comprehensive guidelines for the admissibility of digital evidence in legal proceedings.
ISRO successfully deploys 'Kalpana-3', a next-generation weather satellite, enhancing India's climate monitoring capabilities.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to 'Antyodaya Diwas', consider the following statements:
1. It is observed annually on September 25th.
2. It marks the birth anniversary of Mahatma Gandhi.
3. The 'Antyodaya' principle advocates for uplifting the poorest of the poor.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Antyodaya Diwas is observed on September 25th. Statement 2 is incorrect: It marks the birth anniversary of Pandit Deendayal Upadhyaya, not Mahatma Gandhi (whose birth anniversary is October 2nd). Statement 3 is correct: The Antyodaya principle, integral to Upadhyaya's philosophy, focuses on the welfare and upliftment of the last person in society.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following is NOT a component of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model often cited internationally?
(a) Aadhaar
(b) Unified Payments Interface (UPI)
(c) Goods and Services Tax (GST) Network
(d) DigiLocker
prelimsMocks.explanation: Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker are core components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) or 'India Stack' that are frequently showcased internationally for their role in financial inclusion and digital service delivery. While GST Network is a significant digital platform for taxation, it is not typically grouped under the DPI model in the same context as Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, which focus on identity, payments, and document management for citizens.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Ganges River Dolphin:
1. It is India's National Aquatic Animal.
2. It is found only in the main stem of the Ganga river.
3. It is functionally blind and relies on echolocation.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Ganges River Dolphin is indeed India's National Aquatic Animal. Statement 2 is incorrect: While primarily found in the Ganges, it is also present in the Brahmaputra, Meghna, and Karnaphuli-Sangu river systems in India, Bangladesh, and Nepal. Statement 3 is correct: It is functionally blind, navigating and hunting by emitting ultrasonic sounds and listening for echoes (echolocation).

prelimsMocks.question:
The 'Permanent Indus Commission' (PIC) is a bilateral mechanism established under which of the following treaties?
(a) Shimla Agreement
(b) Tashkent Declaration
(c) Indus Water Treaty
(d) Lahore Declaration
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) was established under the provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT), signed between India and Pakistan in 1960. Its role is to implement and manage the treaty, including exchange of data and resolution of disputes.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) and its missions, the term 'Geostationary Satellite Launch Vehicle (GSLV)' is primarily used for launching:
(a) Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellites for remote sensing.
(b) Satellites into highly elliptical orbits for scientific research.
(c) Heavy communication and meteorological satellites into Geostationary Transfer Orbit (GTO).
(d) Small experimental payloads into polar orbits.
options: (a) Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellites for remote sensing.
(b) Satellites into highly elliptical orbits for scientific research.
(c) Heavy communication and meteorological satellites into Geostationary Transfer Orbit (GTO).
(d) Small experimental payloads into polar orbits.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Heavy communication and meteorological satellites into Geostationary Transfer Orbit (GTO).
prelimsMocks.explanation: The GSLV is primarily designed to launch heavy communication and meteorological satellites into Geostationary Transfer Orbit (GTO). From GTO, the satellite uses its own propulsion system to reach the final geostationary orbit. PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is generally used for LEO and polar orbit missions, including remote sensing satellites.

mainsMocks.question: The 'Antyodaya' principle, championed by Pandit Deendayal Upadhyaya, remains a cornerstone of India's social welfare philosophy. Discuss its relevance in contemporary government policies aimed at inclusive growth and poverty alleviation. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce Antyodaya and Deendayal Upadhyaya. Explain the core idea (uplifting the last person). Connect to contemporary policies like PM-KISAN, Ayushman Bharat, PM-AWAS, DBT. Discuss how these policies aim for last-mile delivery and inclusive growth. Conclude on its continued relevance.

mainsMocks.question: India's consistent call for 'reformed multilateralism' at international forums like the UN General Assembly underscores its evolving global role. Analyze the reasons behind India's demand for reforms in multilateral institutions and the challenges it faces in achieving this objective. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests; Important International institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate.
mainsMocks.approach: Define reformed multilateralism (more representative, democratic, effective). State reasons: outdated structures (UNSC), rise of new powers (India), inability to address contemporary challenges (climate change, terrorism, pandemics), voice for Global South. Discuss challenges: resistance from vested interests (P5 nations), geopolitical rivalries, consensus-based decision making, lack of unified front among developing nations. Conclude on India's strategic vision.

mainsMocks.question: The Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2025, aims to create a robust framework for data privacy in India. Critically examine the challenges in balancing individual privacy rights with the imperatives of national security and economic growth in the context of this legislation. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections. GS3: Basics of cyber security.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Bill and its objective (data privacy). Discuss the 'individual privacy' aspect (consent, data principal rights, DPA). Address 'national security' (government access to data, exemptions, surveillance concerns). Address 'economic growth' (data localization, compliance burden for businesses, innovation vs. regulation). Highlight the balancing act, potential for misuse, and need for clear safeguards. Conclude on the importance of a proportionate and transparent framework.

quickBytes.text: Antyodaya Diwas is celebrated on September 25th, marking the birth anniversary of Pandit Deendayal Upadhyaya.
Integral Humanism, a philosophy advocating for the holistic development of humans and society, was propounded by Deendayal Upadhyaya.
The UN General Assembly (UNGA) is the main deliberative, policymaking, and representative organ of the United Nations.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India has six members, three from the RBI and three appointed by the government.
Section 65B of the Indian Evidence Act, 1872, specifically deals with the admissibility of electronic records as evidence.
Kalpana-1 was India's first dedicated meteorological satellite, launched by ISRO in 2002.
Zero Budget Natural Farming (ZBNF) is a chemical-free agriculture method promoted by Subhash Palekar.
The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution by the Supreme Court in the Puttaswamy judgment (2017).
The Indus Water Treaty (1960) allocates the waters of the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India and Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan, with some exceptions.
Dholavira, a Harappan city in Gujarat, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site known for its sophisticated water management system.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Initiates Major Conservation Project at Ancient Harappan Site
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) commenced a large-scale conservation and restoration project at a prominent ancient Harappan site in Gujarat.
• The project aims to preserve the structural integrity of the excavated remains, protect artifacts, and enhance visitor experience.
• Advanced archaeological techniques, including laser scanning and drone mapping, are being employed for detailed documentation and planning.
• The initiative is part of India's broader commitment to protecting its rich cultural heritage and promoting archaeological research.
• The site, known for its unique urban planning and water management systems, offers crucial insights into the Indus Valley Civilization.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: ASI initiated conservation at a Harappan site in Gujarat.
Uses advanced techniques like laser scanning.
Harappan sites known for urban planning, water systems.
Indus Valley Civilization is one of the world's oldest.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Harappan sites in understanding India's ancient history and cultural heritage.
Analyze the challenges involved in the conservation and preservation of ancient archaeological sites in India.
Evaluate the role of modern technology and community involvement in enhancing the protection and accessibility of such heritage sites.
GS1.imageDescription: Aerial view of an ancient Harappan ruin site, showcasing its distinct urban layout.
GS1.trivia: Dholavira, a Harappan city in Gujarat, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and is particularly known for its sophisticated ancient water management system.

GS2.headline: Antyodaya Diwas: Centre Launches 'Sarvodaya Kalyan Abhiyan' for Integrated Social Welfare
GS2.explainer:
• Coinciding with the birth anniversary of Pandit Deendayal Upadhyaya, the government launched a new integrated welfare program.
• The 'Sarvodaya Kalyan Abhiyan' aims to converge existing flagship schemes like PM-KISAN, Ayushman Bharat, and PM-AWAS.
• Its core focus is on achieving last-mile delivery and ensuring benefits reach the 'last person' (Antyodaya principle).
• The initiative emphasizes technology-driven monitoring, Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT), and community participation for efficient implementation.
• Special attention will be given to identified aspirational districts and vulnerable sections of society to achieve saturation coverage.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
Philosophy of Deendayal Upadhyaya
prelimsPoints: Antyodaya Diwas is celebrated on September 25th.
It marks the birth anniversary of Pandit Deendayal Upadhyaya.
'Sarvodaya Kalyan Abhiyan' aims for convergence of welfare schemes.
Key principles: Antyodaya, last-mile delivery, technology integration.
mainsPoints: Discuss the relevance of Antyodaya principle in contemporary welfare policies.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in converging multiple social welfare schemes.
Evaluate the role of technology and DBT in enhancing the efficiency and transparency of welfare delivery.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration of people from diverse backgrounds receiving benefits from integrated government schemes, symbolizing 'Antyodaya'.
GS2.trivia: Pandit Deendayal Upadhyaya was one of the most important ideologues of the Bharatiya Jana Sangh, the precursor to the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP).

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Climate Justice and Reformed Multilateralism at UN General Assembly
GS2.explainer:
• India's External Affairs Minister addressed the 80th session of the UN General Assembly (UNGA), emphasizing the need for climate justice.
• The address highlighted India's commitment to sustainable development goals while urging developed nations to fulfill their financial and technological commitments.
• India reiterated its call for a reformed multilateralism, advocating for a more representative and equitable United Nations Security Council (UNSC).
• Concerns were raised about rising geopolitical tensions, cross-border terrorism, and the need for collective action to maintain global peace and stability.
• The Minister underscored India's role as a voice for the Global South and its contributions to addressing global challenges.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: UNGA is the main deliberative organ of the UN.
India consistently advocates for UNSC reforms.
Climate justice emphasizes equitable burden-sharing for climate action.
India is a prominent voice for the Global South.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's persistent demand for reforms in multilateral institutions, particularly the UN, and its implications for global governance.
Discuss India's stance on climate change, focusing on the concept of climate justice and its challenges for developing nations.
Evaluate India's role as a leader of the Global South in shaping international discourse on critical issues.
GS2.imageDescription: External Affairs Minister speaking at the podium of the United Nations General Assembly hall.
GS2.trivia: The UN General Assembly convenes annually in September for its regular session, with general debate usually held in the first week.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Lays Down Comprehensive Guidelines for Admissibility of Digital Evidence
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India issued a landmark judgment, establishing detailed guidelines for the admissibility and evidentiary value of digital records in criminal and civil cases.
• The ruling aims to address the complexities arising from the increasing use of electronic evidence and ensure fair trial principles.
• Guidelines cover aspects such as the collection, preservation, authentication, and presentation of digital evidence, including mobile phone data, emails, and CCTV footage.
• The court emphasized the need for specialized training for law enforcement agencies and judicial officers in handling digital forensics.
• This decision is expected to streamline legal proceedings and enhance the reliability of digital evidence in courts across the country.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court ruling on digital evidence admissibility.
Key aspects: collection, preservation, authentication.
Relevant laws: Indian Evidence Act, IT Act.
Impacts criminal and civil cases.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by digital evidence in the Indian legal system and how the Supreme Court's guidelines address them.
Analyze the implications of this judgment for law enforcement agencies and the need for capacity building in digital forensics.
Evaluate the balance struck by the court between technological advancements and ensuring fair trial principles.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal books with a blurred background of a digital screen displaying code.
GS2.trivia: Section 65B of the Indian Evidence Act, 1872, specifically deals with the admissibility of electronic records as evidence.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in Tokyo, with discussions centered on enhancing cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Both nations reaffirmed their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, emphasizing adherence to international law and peaceful resolution of disputes.
• Key areas of cooperation included defense and security, infrastructure development, clean energy, and resilient supply chains.
• Discussions also covered regional security challenges, including maritime security and counter-terrorism efforts.
• The dialogue aimed to strengthen the 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership' between India and Japan, fostering stability and prosperity in the region.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India and Japan hold annual Strategic Dialogue.
Focus on Indo-Pacific region.
Areas of cooperation: defense, infrastructure, clean energy.
Both are members of Quad grouping.
mainsPoints: Examine the significance of the India-Japan Special Strategic and Global Partnership in promoting stability and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss how India's Act East Policy aligns with Japan's vision for a free and open Indo-Pacific.
Analyze the potential for enhanced economic and security cooperation between India and Japan to counter emerging geopolitical challenges.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and Japanese flags displayed side-by-side during a diplomatic meeting.
GS2.trivia: Japan is a significant investor in India's infrastructure, notably involved in projects like the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.

GS2.headline: Joint Parliamentary Committee Submits Report on Digital Personal Data Protection Bill
GS2.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) submitted its report on the highly anticipated Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2025.
• The report includes recommendations for strengthening data privacy safeguards, defining consent mechanisms, and establishing a robust data protection authority.
• Key discussions revolved around the balance between individual privacy rights, government access to data, and the needs of businesses.
• The proposed legislation aims to replace the existing IT Act provisions related to data protection and establish a comprehensive legal framework.
• Experts anticipate the bill to undergo further parliamentary debate before its eventual enactment, marking a significant step for India's digital governance.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Role of Parliament and State Legislatures
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: Digital Personal Data Protection Bill aims to protect privacy.
JPC submitted its report on the bill.
Will replace parts of the IT Act.
Proposes a Data Protection Authority.
mainsPoints: Analyze the importance of a robust data protection framework in the context of India's growing digital economy and citizen rights.
Discuss the challenges in balancing individual privacy, national security, and economic interests in data governance.
Evaluate the recommendations of the JPC and their potential impact on India's digital landscape and fundamental rights.
GS2.imageDescription: A blurred image of parliamentary proceedings with a focus on a document titled 'Data Protection Bill'.
GS2.trivia: The Supreme Court's Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

GS2.headline: New Education Policy: Focus on Foundational Literacy and Numeracy, Implementation Challenges Persist
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education reviewed the progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, with a particular focus on foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN).
• While significant strides have been made in curriculum development and teacher training, challenges in effective implementation at the grassroots level persist.
• Issues such as inadequate infrastructure, teacher vacancies, and digital divide in rural areas were highlighted as key hurdles.
• The government emphasized the need for greater state-level ownership and community participation to achieve NEP's transformative goals.
• Efforts are underway to strengthen early childhood care and education (ECCE) and integrate vocational education from an early stage.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 focuses on FLN and ECCE.
Implementation challenges include infrastructure and teacher vacancies.
Aims to integrate vocational education.
Ministry of Education reviews progress.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the progress and persistent challenges in implementing the National Education Policy 2020, particularly concerning foundational literacy and numeracy.
Discuss the role of state governments and local communities in ensuring the successful realization of NEP's objectives.
Analyze how the NEP addresses issues of equity and access in education, especially in the context of the digital divide.
GS2.imageDescription: Children in a classroom learning, symbolizing foundational education initiatives.
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India, replacing the thirty-four-year-old National Policy on Education (NPE), 1986.

GS2.headline: India-Central Asia Summit Emphasizes Connectivity and Regional Security Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• Leaders from India and five Central Asian nations convened for a virtual summit, focusing on enhancing regional connectivity and security cooperation.
• Discussions centered on optimizing the International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC) and Chabahar Port for seamless trade.
• Both sides expressed concerns over the situation in Afghanistan and its implications for regional stability, pledging coordinated efforts against terrorism.
• Economic cooperation, including trade, investment, and energy partnerships, was identified as a key pillar of the 'extended neighborhood' policy.
• The summit concluded with a joint declaration reaffirming commitment to a strategic partnership based on mutual trust and shared interests.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-Central Asia Summit focuses on connectivity and security.
INSTC and Chabahar Port are key connectivity projects.
Concerns over Afghanistan's stability.
Part of India's 'extended neighborhood' policy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of Central Asia for India's economic and security interests.
Discuss the role of connectivity projects like INSTC and Chabahar Port in strengthening India's engagement with Central Asia.
Evaluate the challenges posed by the situation in Afghanistan to regional security and India's efforts to counter them in cooperation with Central Asian nations.
GS2.imageDescription: Map showing the International North-South Transport Corridor connecting India with Central Asia.
GS2.trivia: The INSTC is a 7,200-km-long multi-modal transport corridor for moving freight between India, Iran, Afghanistan, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Russia, Central Asia, and Europe.

GS2.headline: Indus Water Treaty: Permanent Indus Commission Meeting Concludes with Technical Discussions
GS2.explainer:
• The annual meeting of the Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) between India and Pakistan concluded with constructive technical discussions.
• Delegates reviewed hydrological data, flood forecasting mechanisms, and discussed various projects on the Indus basin rivers.
• While no major breakthroughs were announced, both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT).
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank, governs the sharing of waters of the Indus River System between the two countries.
• Such meetings are crucial for maintaining dialogue and resolving minor technical disputes related to water sharing, despite broader bilateral tensions.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Indus Water Treaty (IWT) between India and Pakistan.
Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) meets annually.
IWT allocates Eastern and Western Rivers.
World Bank is a signatory to the treaty.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and key provisions of the Indus Water Treaty and its significance in India-Pakistan relations.
Analyze the role of the Permanent Indus Commission in resolving water-related disputes and maintaining cooperation despite political tensions.
Evaluate the challenges to the IWT in the face of climate change, increasing water demand, and hydropower development.
GS2.imageDescription: Map of the Indus River System showing the allocation of rivers between India and Pakistan.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Water Treaty, signed in 1960, is considered one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, having survived multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

GS2.headline: PM-Vishwakarma Scheme Empowers Artisans and Craftsmen, Boosts Traditional Skills
GS2.explainer:
• The PM-Vishwakarma Kaushal Samman Yojana, launched to support traditional artisans and craftsmen, shows promising progress in its initial phase.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, skill training, modern tools, and market linkages to 'Vishwakarmas' across 18 traditional trades.
• It aims to preserve India's rich heritage of artisanal skills while integrating these communities into the formal economy.
• Beneficiaries are receiving easy access to credit, digital payment incentives, and branding support for their products.
• The initiative is expected to boost local economies, promote 'Vocal for Local', and empower marginalized sections of society.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
GS2.source: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
prelimsPoints: PM-Vishwakarma scheme for traditional artisans/craftsmen.
Provides financial aid, training, tools, market linkages.
Covers 18 traditional trades.
Aims to preserve heritage and boost local economy.
mainsPoints: Discuss how the PM-Vishwakarma scheme contributes to preserving India's traditional crafts and empowering the artisan community.
Analyze the role of such schemes in promoting 'Vocal for Local' and fostering inclusive economic growth.
Evaluate the challenges faced by traditional artisans and how government interventions can effectively address issues of market access, credit, and skill upgradation.
GS2.imageDescription: An Indian artisan meticulously crafting a traditional product, surrounded by tools and raw materials.
GS2.trivia: The term 'Vishwakarma' refers to the divine architect and craftsman, symbolizing the reverence for traditional skills and craftsmanship in Indian culture.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Key Policy Rates Amidst Global Headwinds, Focus on Inflation Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged.
• The decision was influenced by persistent global economic uncertainties, geopolitical tensions, and domestic inflationary pressures.
• RBI signaled a cautious approach, emphasizing a data-driven strategy to balance growth objectives with inflation control.
• The central bank highlighted the need to monitor food inflation and crude oil prices, which pose significant risks to the economic outlook.
• Liquidity measures were also discussed, with the RBI reaffirming its commitment to maintaining adequate liquidity in the financial system.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC determines key policy rates.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends to commercial banks.
Inflation targeting is a primary objective of MPC.
Global factors like crude oil prices impact domestic inflation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the current challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in managing supply-side inflation and external shocks.
Evaluate the role of the MPC in maintaining financial stability in the Indian economy.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai with a graph showing stable interest rates.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in India in 2016 and consists of six members, with the RBI Governor as its ex-officio chairperson.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Deploys Next-Generation Weather Satellite, Enhancing Climate Monitoring
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched and deployed a state-of-the-art geostationary weather satellite.
• The satellite, designated 'Kalpana-3', is equipped with advanced imaging and atmospheric sounding instruments for improved weather forecasting and climate studies.
• It will provide high-resolution data for monitoring cyclones, monsoons, cloud patterns, and sea surface temperatures.
• This mission significantly boosts India's capability in disaster management, agriculture planning, and understanding regional climate change impacts.
• The launch was carried out using ISRO's Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle (GSLV) from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Space technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO launched 'Kalpana-3' weather satellite.
Uses GSLV launcher.
Provides data for weather, climate, disaster management.
Geostationary orbit for continuous coverage.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of advanced weather satellites for India's agricultural sector and disaster preparedness.
Analyze how ISRO's capabilities in space technology contribute to national development and international cooperation.
Evaluate the role of space-based observations in understanding and mitigating the impacts of climate change in the Indian subcontinent.
GS3.imageDescription: A GSLV rocket launching into space, with a satellite orbiting Earth in the background.
GS3.trivia: Kalpana-1 was India's first dedicated meteorological satellite, launched in 2002 and named after Indian-American astronaut Kalpana Chawla.

GS3.headline: National Mission on Natural Farming to Expand Coverage to New States
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare announced the expansion of the National Mission on Natural Farming (NMNF) to several new states.
• The mission aims to promote chemical-free and sustainable agricultural practices, reducing input costs for farmers and improving soil health.
• Key components include farmer training, establishment of model natural farming villages, and certification of natural farm produce.
• The expansion is expected to boost organic and natural farming, contributing to food security and environmental sustainability.
• Emphasis will be placed on integrating traditional Indian farming practices with modern scientific validation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Agriculture
Government policies and interventions
prelimsPoints: National Mission on Natural Farming (NMNF) promotes chemical-free farming.
Aims to reduce input costs and improve soil health.
Includes farmer training and certification.
Contributes to environmental sustainability.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential benefits and challenges of scaling up natural farming practices across diverse agro-climatic zones in India.
Analyze how natural farming can contribute to India's climate change mitigation and adaptation goals.
Evaluate the role of government policies and farmer awareness in transitioning towards sustainable agricultural systems.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer working in a lush green field, symbolizing natural and sustainable agriculture.
GS3.trivia: Zero Budget Natural Farming (ZBNF) is a popular form of natural farming promoted by agricultural scientist Subhash Palekar.

GS3.headline: India on Track to Exceed Renewable Energy Targets by 2030, Says Ministry Report
GS3.explainer:
• A new report by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy indicates that India is well-positioned to exceed its ambitious renewable energy targets for 2030.
• Significant progress has been made in solar and wind energy capacity additions, driven by policy support and private sector investments.
• The report highlights the role of schemes like the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar PV manufacturing in boosting domestic capabilities.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, energy storage, and land acquisition, but government initiatives are addressing these.
• Achieving these targets is crucial for India's commitment to climate change mitigation and reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Government policies and interventions
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India aims for significant renewable energy capacity by 2030.
Progress driven by solar and wind energy.
PLI scheme supports domestic manufacturing.
Challenges include grid integration and storage.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key policy initiatives and technological advancements that are driving India's renewable energy transition.
Discuss the challenges associated with large-scale renewable energy integration into the national grid and potential solutions.
Evaluate the economic and environmental benefits of India's aggressive renewable energy targets for its long-term sustainable development.
GS3.imageDescription: A large solar power plant stretching to the horizon under a clear sky.
GS3.trivia: India's target under the Paris Agreement is to achieve about 50% cumulative electric power installed capacity from non-fossil fuel-based energy resources by 2030.

GS3.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Garners International Interest at G20 Side Event
GS3.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, including Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, was showcased at a G20 side event.
• Several developing nations expressed keen interest in adopting similar frameworks to accelerate their digital transformation journeys.
• The DPI framework has been lauded for its ability to foster financial inclusion, streamline public service delivery, and boost economic growth.
• Discussions focused on the open-source nature of India's DPI, its interoperability, and the potential for cross-border applications.
• India reaffirmed its commitment to sharing its DPI expertise and experience with other countries, promoting digital cooperation.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
E-governance
prelimsPoints: Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker are components of India's DPI.
DPI model is gaining international interest.
Promotes financial inclusion and public service delivery.
India committed to sharing DPI expertise globally.
mainsPoints: Discuss how India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) has revolutionized financial inclusion and public service delivery in the country.
Analyze the potential for India's DPI model to be adopted by other developing nations and its implications for global digital cooperation.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities associated with scaling up DPI solutions while ensuring data privacy and security.
GS3.imageDescription: Abstract representation of interconnected digital networks with icons of Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
GS3.trivia: Unified Payments Interface (UPI) processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month in India, highlighting its massive adoption.

GS3.headline: National Cybersecurity Strategy: Government Prioritizes Critical Infrastructure Protection
GS3.explainer:
• The National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025 is being actively implemented, with a strong focus on protecting critical information infrastructure (CII).
• Government agencies are collaborating with private sector entities to enhance resilience against sophisticated cyber threats.
• Key initiatives include capacity building for cybersecurity professionals, promoting indigenous cybersecurity products, and fostering international cooperation.
• A new framework for mandatory cyber audits of government and critical private sector entities has been introduced.
• The strategy aims to create a secure and resilient cyberspace for citizens and businesses, safeguarding national security and economic stability.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Internal Security
GS3.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
keyTerms: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsPoints: National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025 focuses on CII protection.
Aims for collaboration between public and private sectors.
Includes capacity building and indigenous product promotion.
Mandatory cyber audits framework introduced.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cyber threats and their implications for India's critical infrastructure and national security.
Analyze the key pillars of India's National Cybersecurity Strategy and the challenges in its effective implementation.
Evaluate the importance of public-private partnerships and international cooperation in building a robust cybersecurity ecosystem.
GS3.imageDescription: Abstract digital network lines protecting a silhouette of a city skyline, symbolizing cybersecurity.
GS3.trivia: The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) is the nodal agency for responding to computer security incidents as and when they occur.

GS3.headline: Ganges River Dolphin Conservation Efforts Intensify, Focus on Habitat Protection
GS3.explainer:
• Conservation efforts for the endangered Ganges River Dolphin have been intensified, with a renewed focus on habitat protection and pollution control.
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) and other agencies are implementing measures to reduce industrial and municipal pollution in key dolphin habitats.
• Community engagement programs are being launched to raise awareness and involve local fishermen in conservation activities.
• Research initiatives are also underway to better understand the dolphin population dynamics, health, and migration patterns.
• The Ganges River Dolphin, India's national aquatic animal, faces threats from habitat degradation, poaching, and accidental entanglement in fishing nets.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Ganges River Dolphin is India's National Aquatic Animal.
Conservation efforts focus on habitat and pollution.
NMCG is involved in conservation.
Threats include pollution, poaching, entanglement.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Ganges River Dolphin and the various threats it faces in its natural habitat.
Analyze the effectiveness of current conservation strategies, including government initiatives and community participation, in protecting this endangered species.
Suggest further measures that can be adopted to ensure the long-term survival of the Ganges River Dolphin amidst increasing anthropogenic pressures.
GS3.imageDescription: A Ganges River Dolphin swimming in clear river water, partially visible above the surface.
